title: NOC 73101 – Tilesetters | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 73101 professionals are the precision finishing experts of the Canadian construction industry. As a TEER 2 occupation, Tilesetters specialize in the high-quality installation of ceramic, glass, marble, and stone tiles on walls, floors, and other surfaces. In 2026, as Canada sees a massive surge in high-end residential towers and luxury commercial hospitality projects, these workers are classified as Critical Finishing Talent. For immigration, this role is a key target for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and several Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) streams.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level2Major Group73 – General tradesSub-Major Group731 – Masonry and plastering tradesMinor Group7310 – Masonry and plastering tradesNOC Code73101

duties: Blueprint Interpretation: Study blueprints and architectural drawings to determine the layout, quantity, and type of materials required.
Surface Preparation: Prepare, measure, and mark surfaces to be covered; build underbeds and install waterproof membranes/backer boards.
Adhesive Mixing: Mix and apply mortar, cement, mastic, glue, or epoxy adhesives using specialized hand trowels.
Precision Setting: Set tiles in position and apply pressure to ensure a secure bond and even surface.
Alignment & Patterning: Align and straighten tiles using levels, squares, chalk lines, and laser lines to achieve perfect geometric patterns.
Cutting & Shaping: Cut and fit tiles around obstacles and openings using hand tools, tile nippers, and power wet saws.
Grouting & Cleaning: Pack grout into joints between tiles and use sponges/mechanical tools to remove excess grout for a clean, professional finish.
Decorative Design: Lay and set mosaic tiles to create decorative wall murals, medallions, and intricate custom floor designs.
Specialized Surfaces: Mix, lay, and polish terrazzo surfaces; cut, shape, and install heavy marble and granite slabs for counters and walls.
Maintenance & Repair: Remove and replace cracked or damaged tiles; prepare detailed material cost estimates for high-end custom projects.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a three-to-four-year apprenticeship program is the standard path to qualification.
Experience: Alternatively, over three years of work experience in the trade and some industry-certified courses may be accepted.
Trade Certification:Compulsory in Quebec.Available but voluntary in ON, AB, BC, MB, NB, NS, PEI, and NL.
Available but voluntary in ON, AB, BC, MB, NB, NS, PEI, and NL.
Red Seal Endorsement: Highly recommended for interprovincial mobility and to maximize points in the Express Entry Federal Skilled Trades Program.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, specialized Tilesetters in urban centers or those working on custom commercial contracts often earn annual salaries between $70,000 and $98,000 inclusive of project bonuses and overtime.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the shift toward sustainable and durable flooring—such as ceramic and stone over carpet—will drive long-term demand. With nearly 25% of the current workforce expected to retire by 2032, new immigrants with specialized tiling skills will find significant job security in the Canadian market.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Tilesetters in 2026 is Strong and Consistent. In British Columbia and Ontario, a record number of luxury condominium completions and hotel refurbishments have kept skilled tilesetters in high demand. According to 2026 Job Bank data, the industry is experiencing a shortage of workers capable of handling large-format porcelain slabs and intricate glass mosaic work. Approximately 88% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time, with a high rate of successful self-employment.
Artistic Precision: Work with world-class architectural designs that emphasize high-end stone and tile aesthetics.
Modern Tooling: Utilize advanced laser alignment systems, electric wet saws, and high-performance adhesives.
Entrepreneurial Opportunities: This trade offers one of the highest paths to self-employment and starting a specialized contracting business in Canada.
Strategic PR Pathways: This role is a priority for the 2026 Trade-Specific Express Entry draws, allowing skilled workers to receive ITAs with competitive CRS scores.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Highly targeted under the Trade Occupations category in 2026.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): A dedicated route for skilled tradespeople with 2 years of experience and a job offer or certificate of qualification.
OINP Skilled Trades Stream: A frequent target for Ontario-based tilesetters to receive a provincial nomination.
Saskatchewan SINP (Hard-to-Fill Skills): A high-priority route for tilesetters with a valid job offer in the province.
